product_brief: Fully integrated, production-ready platform for cryptographic discovery, remediation, and reporting with post-quantum capabilities.
pqc_capability_description: Production-ready, end-to-end PQC software-based solution providing quantum-resilience using quantum secure channels. Supports NIST-approved PQC algorithms. Enables transitions to post-quantum algorithms without code changes or system downtime. Designed for national security, network, IoT, edge devices, and satellite communications.
crypto_primitives: RSA, ECC (mentioned as vulnerable), NIST-approved PQC algorithms (specific algorithm names not stated)
architecture_type: Quantum-security-as-a-service (QSaaS); software-based; supports on-premises or cloud hosting.
release_date: 2023-01-05 (Announcement date for QuProtect R3 launch context)
primary_platforms: Cloud, on-premises, hybrid, air-gapped environments; any device.
target_industries: Telecom, Finance, Defense, Healthcare, Space/Satellite, IT/Data Enterprises, Critical Infrastructure, Government
regulatory_status: Not stated (Aligns with NIST, NSM-10, EO 14028, OMB M-23-02 for compliance reporting)
pqc_roadmap_details: Supports latest NIST-approved PQC algorithms; enables scheduled or on-demand transitions to post-quantum algorithms.
signature_schemes: Not stated (General mention of RSA, ECC, and NIST-approved PQC algorithms)

product_brief: Unified platform for AI security and cryptographic posture management, including PQC migration planning.
pqc_capability_description: The platform provides continuous cryptographic inventory, assesses risk, and plans post-quantum migration. It includes PQC impact simulation built directly into the platform. Researchers contributed to NIST post-quantum cryptography standards.
primary_platforms: Cloud (SaaS); specific cloud providers not stated.
pqc_roadmap_details: Includes PQC impact simulation and planning for post-quantum migration; aligns with NIST standards.

product_brief: High-performance quantum-safe software development kit for constrained devices to large-scale cloud applications.
pqc_support: Yes (Production-ready; supports ML-KEM, ML-DSA, and NIST-standardized PQC algorithms)
pqc_capability_description: Production-ready SDK offering modular libraries for integrating PQC algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA). Supports hybrid certificates for migration. Achieves NIST PQC Security Level 5 on resource-limited devices. Includes MISRA-C compliant code and tools for validation of performance and interoperability between legacy and upgraded systems.
crypto_primitives: ML-KEM, ML-DSA (NIST-standardized PQC algorithms); Hybrid certificates mentioned; Legacy cryptography implied but specific classical primitives not explicitly named in text.
architecture_type: Software Development Kit (SDK) with Embedded and Standard variants; supports integration into embedded systems, IoT, enterprise software, and cloud applications.
license: Proprietary (Commercial implied by "Book Your Meeting", "ISARA Advance®", and lack of open source license mention)
primary_platforms: Embedded devices, IoT, smartcards, general-purpose devices, enterprise software, large-scale cloud applications; compatible with OpenSSL/NSS.
target_industries: Financial Services, Government & Public Sector, Energy & Utilities
regulatory_status: Mentions compliance with DORA, GDPR, PCI DSS, CNSA 2.0 requirements, and CISA initiatives.
pqc_roadmap_details: Supports transition to NIST-standardized PQC algorithms (ML-KEM, ML-DSA); promotes crypto agility via simple APIs for algorithm switching; plans for hybrid then fully quantum-safe PKI transitions.

product_brief: Automated cryptographic discovery and risk prioritisation tool for post-quantum migration planning.
pqc_capability_description: Provides complete cryptographic inventory, continuous discovery, and risk prioritisation to plan and execute migration to post-quantum cryptography (PQC). Validates methodology via UK NCSC Post-Quantum Cryptography Pilot. Does not explicitly state specific PQC algorithms implemented within the tool itself, but focuses on discovering legacy algorithms to enable migration.
crypto_primitives: Not stated (Document mentions "legacy algorithms" and "deprecated protocols" generally but does not list specific primitives like ECDSA or RSA).
architecture_type: Not stated (Described as a product delivering inventory and discovery, likely SaaS, but architecture details are not specified).
primary_platforms: Networks, applications, and cloud services (specific providers not listed).
regulatory_status: Selected by UK National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) for Post-Quantum Cryptography Pilot under the Assured Cyber Security Consultancy Scheme.
pqc_roadmap_details: Focuses on exposing hidden encryption risks, accelerating secure migration via roadmap insights, and continuous monitoring; specific algorithm choices or timelines not stated.
